description: Drive PR #1 (refactor: move expandCellSpec to lib/warehouse/codes) to MERGED by unblocking the Verify CI workflow and locking in the moved function's behaviour with a verifier script.

userStories.title: Stub DATABASE_URL for prisma generate in verify.yml
userStories.description: As the PR #1 author, I want the Verify workflow's `Generate Prisma client` step to succeed without a real database, so that the PR's required CI check turns green and merge becomes possible.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/verify.yml` step `Generate Prisma client` (currently at lines 25-28) gains a step-scoped `env:` block setting `DATABASE_URL` to an obviously-stubbed value such as `postgresql://prisma-generate-stub:stub@localhost:5432/stub`.
The `env:` block is on the step, NOT on the job — subsequent steps (`Type check`, `Lint`) must NOT see this stub value.

The inline comment at line 27 (`No DATABASE_URL needed for prisma generate; it reads the schema only.`) is updated to explain that the stub is required because `prisma.config.ts:14` calls `env("DATABASE_URL")` which throws when unset — not a real DB connection.
No edits to `prisma.config.ts`, `package.json`, or any non-CI file in this story.
After pushing to branch `archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869`, the `verify` check on PR #1 reports `pass` (`gh pr checks 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka` shows `verify  pass`).
Type-check passes locally: `npx tsc --noEmit`.
userStories.technicalNotes:
Edit `.github/workflows/verify.yml`. Insert an `env:` block under the `Generate Prisma client` step (between `# Required so tsc can resolve...` comment block and `run: npx prisma generate`). YAML pattern:
```yaml
      - name: Generate Prisma client
        env:
          # prisma.config.ts:14 calls env("DATABASE_URL") which throws when unset.
          # `prisma generate` only reads the schema and never connects, so any
          # non-empty value satisfies the env() resolver.
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://prisma-generate-stub:stub@localhost:5432/stub
        run: npx prisma generate
```
Root cause confirmed at `prisma.config.ts:14` (`url: env("DATABASE_URL")`) and the failing run log `https://github.com/0pt1m1sm/geleoteka/actions/runs/27423993649/job/81056748222` line `Missing required environment variable: DATABASE_URL`. Push via `git push origin HEAD:archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869`. Verify CI with `gh pr checks 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka --watch`.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 1. Files: .github/workflows/verify.yml. Commit 60306c6 was made on the PR head branch archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869 in sibling worktree and pushed to origin. Stub DATABASE_URL is scoped to the Generate Prisma client step only. Locally verified `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://prisma-generate-stub:stub@localhost:5432/stub npx prisma generate` succeeds and unstubbed run fails with `Missing required environment variable: DATABASE_URL`. Type-check passes.

userStories.title: Add scripts/verify-cell-codes.ts to lock in expandCellSpec behaviour
userStories.description: As the PR #1 author, I want a runnable verifier for `expandCellSpec` so that the PR's stated motivation (`the function was un-unit-testable without the Next.js runtime`) is realised as a delivered artifact and any future refactor of `lib/warehouse/codes.ts` (the deferred `lib/warehouse/` rename) has a regression baseline.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `scripts/verify-cell-codes.ts` exists on branch `archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869`.

The file follows the verifier shape established by `scripts/verify-stock-analysis.ts:1-60`: `import "dotenv/config"` at top, a local `assert(cond, msg)` helper that `console.error`s and `process.exit(1)`, a single `main()` invoked at module bottom with `.catch(...)`.
It imports `expandCellSpec` and `CELL_RE` from `../lib/warehouse/codes` (same import-path convention as `scripts/verify-warehouse.ts:23`).
No DB code: no `db` import, no fixtures, no cleanup — `expandCellSpec` is pure.

Positive cases asserted: `expandCellSpec("A-1-1")` returns `["A-1-1"]`; `expandCellSpec("A-1-1..A-1-3")` returns `["A-1-1","A-1-2","A-1-3"]`; `expandCellSpec(" a-1-1 .. A-1-2 ")` returns `["A-1-1","A-1-2"]` (case + whitespace normalisation).
2-D range covered: `expandCellSpec("A-1-1..A-2-2")` returns `["A-1-1","A-1-2","A-2-1","A-2-2"]` (cartesian product over differing numeric segments).

Negative cases asserted with the exact Russian strings already in `lib/warehouse/codes.ts`: empty spec → `"Укажите код или диапазон ячеек"`; mismatched segment count → `"Диапазон: коды должны иметь одинаковую структуру"`; oversized numeric range (e.g. `A-0..A-501`) → `"Слишком большой диапазон"`; oversized product (range whose cartesian explodes past 1000) → `"Слишком много ячеек (макс. 1000)"`; non-numeric range segment (e.g. `A-1..B-1`) → `"Сегмент 1: перебор возможен только по числам"`.
`CELL_RE` smoke-tested: matches `"A-1-1"`, rejects `"a-1"` (lowercase), rejects 33-char string.
Script runs cleanly: `npx tsx scripts/verify-cell-codes.ts` exits 0 with no DB connection required.
Script fails loudly when an assertion breaks (verified by temporarily inverting one assertion during development — restore before commit).
Type-check passes: `npx tsc --noEmit`. Lint passes: `npm run lint`.
userStories.notes: Implemented in iteration 2. Files: scripts/verify-cell-codes.ts (new, on PR branch archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869). Commit ff5feaa pushed to origin. Verifier exits 0 locally; loud-fail verified by temporarily inverting one assertion (FAIL: single code body, exit 1) then restored. Type-check + lint clean.

userStories.title: Merge PR #1 once Verify is green
userStories.description: As the repository maintainer, I want PR #1 squash-merged with its branch deleted, so that the architectural-sweep baseline lands on `main` and unblocks the next deferred follow-up PRs (`lib/warehouse/` rename, `generateTempPassword` dedup, typed-delegate shim, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Before merging: `gh pr checks 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka` reports `verify  pass`.

Before merging: `gh pr view 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka --json mergeable,mergeStateStatus` reports `"mergeable":"MERGEABLE"` and `"mergeStateStatus":"CLEAN"` (or `"UNSTABLE"` only if the failing check is one we explicitly don't gate on — there is none).
Merge executed with: `gh pr merge 1 --squash --delete-branch --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka`. Squash matches the existing one-commit-per-feature history (`acadba6`, `61d1f04`, `ed34535`, …).
Post-merge: `gh pr view 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka --json state` reports `"state":"MERGED"`.
Post-merge: branch `archon/task-archon-architect-1781275339869` is gone from `gh api repos/0pt1m1sm/geleoteka/branches --jq '.[].name'`.
Post-merge: `git log origin/main -1 --format='%s'` shows the squashed commit title contains `expandCellSpec` (or the PR title verbatim).
userStories.technicalNotes:
Do NOT use `--admin` or any force flag. Do NOT skip checks. If `Verify` is not yet green when this story runs, BLOCK — return control to US-001/US-002 rather than waiting indefinitely or attempting a merge override.

Merge command (verbatim):
```bash
gh pr merge 1 --squash --delete-branch --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka
```
Verification command (verbatim):
```bash
gh pr view 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit
```
If `gh pr merge` fails with `Pull request is not mergeable` despite green checks, inspect with `gh pr view 1 --repo 0pt1m1sm/geleoteka --json mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision` and STOP — do not work around. Most likely cause would be a base-branch divergence (rebase needed) or a missing review approval; both are out of this story's scope to resolve.

Do NOT proactively run `git push --force` to PR #1's branch under any circumstance — it would break the review trail. Squash is the merge strategy, NOT a pre-merge rebase.
